user_query:
I'm a young soccer player keeping my training logs locally with a tiny script, but it recently started crashing and stopped writing new stats. Please help me do a quick incident response and reliability fix using the files in this workspace.

Inputs to inspect:
- Code: app/tracker.py
- Config: app/config.json
- Logs: logs/run-2026-04-17.log
- Sensor sample data: sensors/mock_heart_rate.csv

What I need:
1) Identify the root cause of the crash by inspecting the code, config, and the provided log file. Focus on why the script fails before reading sensor data.
2) Apply a reliability fix so the script can handle this case and pass a basic check. You may either:
   - Modify app/tracker.py to robustly parse the polling interval when it includes a "ms" suffix, or
   - Correct app/config.json to use a numeric millisecond value for sensor_poll_interval_ms.
   Pick one approach and make the change; leave other behavior intact.
3) After your fix, run: python app/tracker.py --check. This should write a status file at out/status.json. The status must be "ok" after your changes.
4) Write a structured incident report to out/incident_report.json with the following fields:
   - root_cause: a short string explaining the failure and the specific key/value involved.
   - evidence: an object with:
     - log_excerpt: a copied line from logs/run-2026-04-17.log that shows the exception (must include the word "ValueError").
     - file_paths_inspected: ["app/tracker.py", "app/config.json", "logs/run-2026-04-17.log"]
   - fix: an object with:
     - approach: "code_change" or "config_change"
     - files_modified: array of file paths you changed
     - changes: for a config change, include old and new values of sensor_poll_interval_ms; for a code change, include the function name you altered and a 1-2 sentence summary of the change
   - post_fix_check: an object with:
     - status_file: "out/status.json"
     - status: the status value read from out/status.json (not hardcoded)

Constraints and verification:
- Do not add new dependencies. Use only the provided files and standard Python.
- Do not change file names or output paths.
- Make sure out/status.json and out/incident_report.json are created.
- Your fix must be reflected in the modified file(s) so I can review the change.

Goal: I want the script's basic check to pass reliably and a clear report of what failed and how it was fixed, so I can keep logging my training without crashes. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
